title: Small bone orthopedic implants
abstract: Dual threaded orthopedic implants (10, 10a, 40, 40a, 60, 100, 350) described herein are used for fractures, inter-digital fusion of the fingers, toes, and other small bones of the body, as well as other procedures. Each orthopedic implant (10, 10a, 40, 40a, 60, 100, 350) has first and second implant ends and first and second coupling/insertion ends (12, 14; 42, 48; 67, 69; 250, 260; 352, 354). The implant may comprise a single component (40, 40a, 60, 100) or two components (10, 10a, 350). External threading is provided on the implant ends. In the single component form (40, 40a, 60, 100), the single component includes the first and second drive ends, while in the two component form (10, 10a, 350), a first component (12, 14; 12, 14a; 352, 354) has the first drive end and a second component has the second drive end. In the two component version, a head (18, 359) of one component (12, 352) receives an end (33,375) of the other component (14, 14a, 354) to provide coupling or joining of the two components.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1 . An orthopedic implant (10, 10a, 40, 40a, 60, 100, 350) for treating small bones of the hand and foot comprising: a component (10) having a first segment (12, 42, 67, 250, 352) and a second segment (14, 48, 69, 260, 354); first external threading (16, 43, 63, 270, 358) on the first segment of the component; and second external threading (27, 44, 64, 290, 373) on the second segment of the component. 2. The orthopedic implant (10, 10a, 40, 40a, 60, 100, 350) of claim 1 , wherein: the first external threading (16, 43, 63, 270, 358) provides rotation in a first direction; the second external threading (27, 44, 64, 290, 373) provides rotation in a second direction; and the first and second directions are opposite one another. 3. The orthopedic implant (10, 10a, 40, 40a, 60, 100, 350) of claim 1 , wherein the first segment (12, 42, 67, 250, 352) and the second segment (14, 48, 69, 260, 354) are one member. 4. The orthopedic implant (10, 10a, 40, 40a, 60, 100, 350) of claim 3, wherein the first segment and the second segment are separated by a break-away portion that allows the first segment to be separated from the second segment. 5. The orthopedic implant (10, 10a, 40, 40a, 60, 100, 350) of claim 1 , wherein the first segment and the second segment are separate first (12, 352) and second (14, 354) members. 6. The orthopedic implant (10, 10a, 40, 40a, 60, 100, 350) of claim 5, wherein: the first member (12, 352) has a first end and a second end, the first end of the first member having a head (18, 359); and 

 the second member (14, 354) has a first end and a second end, the first end of the second member having a boss (33, 375) configured for joining reception with the head of the first end of the first member. 7. The orthopedic implant (10, 10a, 40, 40a, 60, 100, 350) of claim 6, wherein: the second end of the first member has a first generally planar bone cutting configuration (659); and the second end of the second member has a second generally planar bone cutting configuration (680). 8. The orthopedic implant (10, 10a, 40, 40a, 60, 100, 350) of claim 6, wherein: the second end of the first member has a first generally pointed bone cutting configuration (17); and the second end of the second member has a second generally pointed bone cutting configuration (28). 9. The orthopedic implant (10, 10a, 40, 40a, 60, 100, 350) of claim 6, wherein the head of the first end of the first member has a socket (21 , 363) configured to receive the boss (33, 375) of the first end of the second member. 10. The orthopedic implant (10, 10a, 40, 40a, 60, 100, 350) of claim 9, wherein a wall (20, 362) defining the head of the first end of the first member includes an inner radial ledge (22, 670) configured to receive a connection structure on the first end of the second member. 1 1 . The orthopedic implant (10, 10a, 40, 40a, 60, 100, 350) of claim 10, wherein the connection structure on the first end of the second member comprises radially extending tangs (34, 380). 12. The orthopedic implant (10, 10a, 40, 40a, 60, 100, 350) of claim 10, wherein the wall has a plurality of vertical slots (19, 360/361 ). 13. An orthopedic implant (10, 10a, 40, 40a, 60, 100, 350) for use in treating small bones of the body comprising: 

 a component (41 ) having a shaft (47) with a first end and a second end; first threading (16, 43, 63, 270, 358) proximate the first end of the shaft; second threading (27, 44, 64, 290, 373) proximate the second end of the shaft; a tip (42) on the first end of the shaft (47); and a driver end (45) on the second end configured to receive a driver. 14. The orthopedic implant (10, 10a, 40, 40a, 60, 100, 350) of claim 13, wherein the second end of the shaft (47) has a diameter that is greater than the first end of the shaft. 15. An orthopedic implant (10, 10a, 100, 350) for treating small bones of the hand and foot comprising: a first component (12, 352) defining a first end and a second end; a head (18, 359) disposed at the first end of the first component; a second component (14, 14a, 354) defining a first end and a second end, the first end of the second member having a boss (33, 375) configured for joining reception with the head of the first end of the first component; first external threading (16, 358) on the first component, the first external threading providing insertion rotation of a first direction; and second external threading (27, 373) on the second component, the second external threading providing insertion rotation of a second direction, the first and second insertion directions opposite one another. 16. The orthopedic implant (10, 10a, 40, 40a, 60, 100, 350) of claim 15, wherein: the second end of the first component has a first generally planar bone cutting configuration (659); and the second end of the second component has a second generally planar bone cutting configuration (680). 17. The orthopedic implant (10, 10a, 40, 40a, 60, 100, 350) of claim 15, wherein: the second end of the first component has a first generally pointed bone cutting configuration (17); and 

 the second end of the second component has a second generally pointed bone cutting configuration (28). 18. The orthopedic implant (10, 10a, 40, 40a, 60, 100, 350) of claim 17, wherein: the head (18, 359) of the first end of the first component has a socket () configured to receive the boss of the first end of the second member; and a wall (20, 362) defining the head of the first end of the first component includes an inner radial ledge (22, 670) configured to receive a connection structure on the first end of the second component, the wall having a plurality of vertical slots (19, 360/361 ). 19. The orthopedic implant (10, 10a, 40, 40a, 60, 100, 350) of claim 18, wherein the connection structure on the first end of the second component comprises radially extending tangs (34, 380).
